ab: koko’ ’ay taew’an ay?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: ’ihi’, siya ray pino’azisan mae’rem.
en: Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

ab: koko’ ray taew’an ay?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: ’oka’, baba:aw siya kaSlatar ila, hae:wan ila ’am lobih. .
ch: B:不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
en: No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

ab: baki’ ’ay taew’an ay?
en: Is grandfather at home?

ab: siya ray minaypoSal tinaew’an, So’o Sa’ila ki:im hi siya.
ch: B:他在二樓，你上去找他吧。
en: He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.

ab: hayza’ ay ka mae’iyaeh ray taew’an?
en: Is anyone at home?

ab: ’ataw o, honaehnge: ila ’okay kita’ ’iSo’on. So’o kayzaeh ay?
ch: B:是'ataw阿，很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
en: It’s ’ataw. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

ab: kayzaeh a! ya:o mowa:i’ ’am ’a’iyalatar ki So’o.
en: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

ab: yaba’ niSo’ hayno’?
en: Where is your father?

ab: siya ray katalekan homangra: ka tatimae’, ya:o takobae:aeh hi siya.
ch: B:他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
en: He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.

ab: ’away ’ila’ino’? ?
en: Where did ’away go?

ab: siya rima’ ray kawnatata:a’an romakep ka tata:a’.
en: She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

ab: baki’, yami lobih ila.
en: Grandfather, we’re here.

ab: nimon baki’ ’okik ’ay taew’an, siya ray pinatiyayan, moyo ’ay taew’an ’ayna:a’ hi siya.
ch: B:你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他吧。
en: Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.

ab: mama’ So’o mowa:i’ ila!
en: Uncle, you’re here!

ab: honaehnge: ila ’okay kakita’, So’o ’ibabaw ila.
en: I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.

ab: komita’ So’o mowa:i’ yako kin Siyae’.
en: It’s so good to see you.

ab: hini pangrang ’inimon, ’aSkan ray kapak’iya’zawan.
ch: B:這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
en: Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

ab: honaehnge: ila ’okay wa:i’, ya:o takobae:aeh ka saboeh mowa:i’ ma’onhael somi’ael.
ch: A:這麼久沒來，我叫大家一起來吃飯。
en: It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

ab: ’izi’ ila patawaw, ya:o milkashaw ma’ kayzaeh ila, ’ita’ ta’a’iyalatar ray kinbaki’an.
ch: B:不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
en: No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.

ab: ma’alo’ ila ’iSo’on, yako ’am lobih ila.
en: Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

ab: hini tata:a’ baba:aw hiwa’en, be:ayen ’iniSo’ ’alibihin si’aelen.
ch: B:這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
en: This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.
